info.title: GitHub REST API (stripped for pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples discovery)
info.description: A minimal subset of the GitHub REST API exposing only the read-only, fully unauthenticated endpoints needed to discover and browse declarative agent samples in the pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples repository. All endpoints in this spec return 200 anonymously. The auth-only `GET /search/code` endpoint has been intentionally omitted — discovery is driven by the structured `samples.json` registry instead. All endpoints share GitHub's per-IP unauthenticated rate limit (~60 req/hour overall; the Search API additionally caps unauthenticated calls at ~10 req/minute).

get.summary: Get the structured registry of every sample in pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples.
get.description: Returns the contents API response for `.github/samples.json` — a hand-maintained, ~100 KB JSON array describing every sample in the repo. The response is a standard contents object with a base64-encoded `content` field; decode it and parse as JSON to get the array. Each registry entry includes `name`, `title`, `shortDescription`, `longDescription`, `Folder` (the folder name under `samples/`), `metadata` (an array of `{ key, value }` pairs — keys include `PLATFORM`, `LANGUAGE`, `API-PLUGIN`, `GRAPH-CONNECTOR`, etc.), `thumbnails`, and `authors`. THIS IS THE PRIMARY DISCOVERY TOOL — always call it first for any 'which samples', 'find a sample', 'show me samples that…' question.

get.summary: Get the full recursive file tree of pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples in one anonymous call.
get.description: Returns the full recursive Git tree for the `pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples` repo in a SINGLE anonymous call (~3,000+ entries, ~860 KB, `truncated: false` as of 2026-06-12). Pass `tree_sha` = `main` (the default branch, recommended) or any branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Set `recursive=1` to traverse every subdirectory. The response contains `tree[]` where each entry has `path` (full path from repo root), `type` (`blob` for files, `tree` for directories), `sha`, `size` (blobs only), and `url`. USE THIS for path-pattern questions: 'which samples ship an `ai-plugin.json`?', 'find every Python file under samples/', 'list every `m365agents.yml`', 'which samples have a `M365Agent/appPackage/` folder?'. Filter the `tree` array client-side by `path` glob; then call `getRepoContent` ONLY for the specific files you decide to read. Never iterate every blob with `getRepoContent` (rate limit).

parameters.name: tree_sha
parameters.description: Tree reference. Use `main` for the default branch (recommended), or any branch name, tag, or commit SHA.

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Set to `1` to traverse every subdirectory and return the full repo file tree in one call (recommended). Omit for a shallow listing of the root tree only.

422.description: Tree too large to return (would only happen if the repo grew enormously).

get.summary: List every top-level sample folder under `samples/` in pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples.
get.description: Returns an array of directory entries for the top-level `samples/` folder. Use this as a sanity check or fallback when the structured `samples.json` registry might be stale or missing a brand-new sample. Each entry's `name` is the sample folder name (e.g., `da-ristorante-api-csharp`, `da-pnp-samples-guide`).

200.description: Array of sample folder directory entries.

get.description: Search public repositories on GitHub. Use ONLY for cross-repo discovery ('what other PnP repos exist for X', 'find Microsoft 365 Copilot repos by topic'). Do NOT use for in-repo sample discovery — call `getSamplesRegistry` for that. Anonymous limit: ~10 requests/minute.
Returns metadata about a public repository (description, stars, default branch, license, topics, etc.). Typical use: owner=`pnp`, repo=`copilot-pro-dev-samples`.

get.summary: Get the contents of a file or directory in a public repo.
get.description: Returns the contents of a file (base64-encoded) or directory listing. Use this AFTER `getSamplesRegistry` has identified a specific sample, to read that sample's `README.md`, `declarativeAgent.json`, or `ai-plugin.json`. For text files, decode the base64 `content` field. For directories, the response is a JSON array of entries. Typical use: owner=`pnp`, repo=`copilot-pro-dev-samples`, path=`samples/<Folder>/README.md`.

Returns the top-level README of a public repository as a file content object (base64-encoded). Decode `content` to read the markdown. Use for repo-level overview, not for individual sample READMEs (call `getRepoContent` for those).

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded. Anonymous overall limit is ~60 req/hour per IP; the Search API additionally caps unauthenticated calls at ~10 req/minute.

FileContent.description: Standard GitHub contents-API response for a single file.

content.description: Base64-encoded file content. Decode before parsing or display.

GitTree.description: GitHub Git Tree response. The full recursive tree for pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples currently fits in one call (`truncated: false`).

truncated.description: True only when GitHub had to truncate the result. Currently false for pnp/copilot-pro-dev-samples.

path.description: Full path from repo root, e.g. `samples/da-pnp-samples-guide/M365Agent/appPackage/ai-plugin.json`.

size.description: Size in bytes (present on `blob` entries only).
